message.content:
Based on my exploration, I need to design an implementation to change the "Salvar PDF" button behavior from opening HTML in a new tab to directly downloading a PDF file.

## Current System Context:

**Current Implementation:**
- File: `/components/proposal-document.tsx`
- Function: `handleDownloadPDF()` (lines 62-105)
- Current behavior: Fetches template HTML, replaces {{tokens}}, opens new window, triggers print dialog
- User must manually "Save as PDF" with correct margins

**Available Resources:**
- jsPDF library already installed (`jspdf@^2.5.1`)
- Well-structured token system in `/lib/proposal-pdf-utils.ts`
- HTML template at `/public/proposta-template.html` (553 lines, print-optimized)
- Function `buildProposalTokens()` creates all template variables

**User Requirement:**
- Button should directly save/download a PDF file
- No manual user intervention required
- Maintain the same visual output as current HTML template

## Design Requirements:

1. **Preserve existing functionality:** Keep the same template system and token building
2. **Direct PDF download:** No new tabs, no print dialogs
3. **Automatic filename:** Generate meaningful filename (e.g., "Proposta_ClienteName_Date.pdf")
4. **Same visual output:** PDF should look identical to current HTML output
5. **Error handling:** Graceful handling of PDF generation errors
6. **Performance:** Reasonable generation time for user experience

## Technical Constraints:
- Client-side implementation preferred (since current is client-side)
- Must work with existing template variables system
- Should maintain the current button UI and loading states
- Need to handle complex HTML with CSS (fonts, layout, colors)

## Design Options to Consider:

**Option A:** HTML-to-PDF using jsPDF + html2canvas
**Option B:** Pure jsPDF with manual layout recreation
**Option C:** Server-side PDF generation with API endpoint
**Option D:** html2pdf.js library (additional dependency)

Please provide a detailed implementation plan considering:
1. Which technical approach is most suitable
2. Code changes required in proposal-document.tsx
3. Any additional dependencies needed
4. Step-by-step implementation approach
5. Error handling strategy
6. Testing approach

content.text: I'll design a comprehensive implementation plan to change the "Salvar PDF" button from opening HTML to directly downloading a PDF file. Let me first explore the current codebase to understand the existing architecture.

Busca o novo template finalizado 67 const res = await fetch("/proposta-template.html") 68 if (!res.ok) throw new Error("Template não encontrado") 69 let html = await res.text() 70 71 // 2. Constrói os tokens com o novo sistema de mapeamento 72 const tokens = buildProposalTokens({ empresa, para, projeto, modeloTrabalho, dataCriacao, sections }) 73 console.log("[ProposalDocument] Generated tokens:", tokens) 74 console.log("[ProposalDocument] Sections:", sections) 75 76 // 3. Substitui todas as variáveis no HTML 77 for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(tokens)) { 78 html = html.replace(new RegExp(`\\{\\{${key}\\}\\}`, 'g'), value || '') 79 } 80 81 // 4. Remove variáveis não utilizadas (novo sistema de limpeza) 82 html = html.replace(/\{\{[A-Z_]+\}\}/g, '') 83 84 // 5. Abre em nova janela e dispara impressão automaticamente 85 const win = window.open("", "_blank") 86 if (!win) { 87 toast.error("Pop-up bloqueado. Permita pop-ups para este site e tente novamente.") 88 return 89 } 90 win.document.open() 91 win.document.write(html) 92 win.document.close() 93 94 // Aguarda carregamento completo antes de imprimir 95 win.addEventListener("load", () => win.print()) 96 setTimeout(() => { if (win && !win.closed) win.print() }, 800) 97 98 toast.success("Proposta aberta! Selecione 'Salvar como PDF' com margens 'Nenhuma'.") 99 } catch (err) { 100 console.error("[ProposalDocument] PDF error:", err) 101 toast.error("Erro ao gerar o PDF.") 102 } finally { 103 setDownloading(false) 104 } 105 } 106 107 function getStatusBadge() { 108 switch (status) { 109 case "Aprovada": 110 return Aprovada 111 case "Concluída": 112 return Concluída 113 case "Rascunho": 114 default: 115 return Rascunho 116 } 117 } 118 119 return ( 120

1 import type { ProposalSection, ProposalModelo } from "@/lib/proposal-types" 2 import { formatDateShort } from "@/lib/proposal-types" 3 4 export function clean(text: string): string { 5 return text.replace(/\*\*/g, "").replace(/\*/g, "").trim() 6 } 7 8 export function findSection(sections: ProposalSection[], ...keywords: string[]): ProposalSection | undefined { 9 return sections.find((s) => 10 keywords.some((k) => 11 s.key.toLowerCase().includes(k.toLowerCase()) || 12 s.title.toLowerCase().includes(k.toLowerCase()) || 13 s.content.toLowerCase().includes(k.toLowerCase()) 14 ) 15 ) 16 } 17 18 // ── GERAÇÃO DE HTML ESTRUTURADO ── 19 20 export function gerarFuncionalidadesHTML(content: string): string { 21 const lines = content.split("\n") 22 const items: string[] = [] 23 24 for (const line of lines) { 25 const t = line.trim() 26 // Formato: "1. **Nome** — Descrição" 27 const m1 = t.match(/^(\d+)\.\s+\*\*(.+?)\*\*\s*[—–:\-]\s*(.+)$/) 28 if (m1) { 29 items.push(`

${m1[1]}. ${m1[2]} - ${clean(m1[3])}

`) 30 continue 31 } 32 33 // Formato: "1. Nome — Descrição" 34 const m2 = t.match(/^(\d+)\.\s+(.+?)\s*[—–]\s*(.+)$/) 35 if (m2) { 36 items.push(`

${m2[1]}. ${m2[2]} - ${clean(m2[3])}

`) 37 continue 38 } 39 40 // Formato: "- **Nome**: Descrição" 41 const m3 = t.match(/^[-*•]\s+\*\*(.+?)\*\*[:\s]+(.+)$/) 42 if (m3) { 43 items.push(`

${items.length + 1}. ${m3[1]} - ${clean(m3[2])}

`) 44 continue 45 } 46 } 47 48 // Fallback: itens simples com bullet points 49 if (!items.length) { 50 content.split("\n") 51 .filter((l) => /^[-*•]\s/.test(l.trim())) 52 .slice(0, 8) 53 .forEach((l, i) => { 54 items.push(`

${i + 1}. ${clean(l.replace(/^[-*•]\s+/, ""))}

`) 55 }) 56 } 57 58 return items.join("\n\n") 59 } 60 61 export function gerarFasesHTML(content: string): string { 62 const lines = content.split("\n") 63 const phases: string[] = [] 64 let counter = 0 65 66 for (const line of lines) { 67 const t = line.trim() 68 if (!t) continue 69 70 // Formato: "Fase 1 - Nome | Prazo" 71 const m1 = t.match(/^(?:Fase\s*)?(\d+)\s*[-:]\s*(.+?)\s*\|\s*(.+)$/) 72 if (m1) { 73 phases.push(`
Fase ${m1[1]}: ${clean(m1[2])} – ${clean(m1[3])}
`) 74 continue 75 } 76 77 // Formato: "Fase 1 - Nome" 78 const m2 = t.match(/^Fase\s*(\d+)\s*[-:]\s*(.+)$/) 79 if (m2) { 80 phases.push(`
Fase ${m2[1]}: ${clean(m2[2])}
`) 81 continue 82 } 83 84 // Bullets com prazo 85 if (/^[-*•]\s/.test(t) && /semana|mês|mes|dias?/i.test(t)) { 86 counter++ 87 phases.push(`
Fase ${counter}: ${clean(t.replace(/^[-*•]\s+/, ""))}
`) 88 } 89 } 90 91 return phases.join("\n") || `
Fase 1: A definir
` 92 } 93 94 export function gerarEntregaveisHTML(content: string): string { 95 if (content.trim()) { 96 // Se há conteúdo, processar línhas que parecem ser entregáveis 97 const lines = content.split("\n") 98 const items: string[] = [] 99 100 for (const line of lines) { 101 const trimmed = line.trim() 102 // Buscar por linhas com entregáveis específicos 103 if (/^[-*•]\s/.test(trimmed) || /^(protótipo|documentação|código|sistema|arquivo|suporte)/i.test(trimmed)) { 104 const item = clean(trimmed.replace(/^[-*•]\s+/, "")) 105 if (item.length > 5) { // Evitar itens muito curtos 106 items.push(`
  • ${item}
    • `) 107 } 108 } 109 } 110 111 if (items.length > 0) { 112 return `
      \n${items.join("\n")}\n
    ` 113 } 114 } 115 116 // Lista padrão de entregáveis 117 return `

"Projeto") 195 } 196 197 export function extractDescricao(content: string): string { 198 const lines = content.split("\n") 199 const paragrafos = lines.filter((l) => { 200 const t = l.trim() 201 return t && !t.startsWith("#") && !t.startsWith("-") && !t.startsWith("*") && !/^\d+\./.test(t) 202 }).map(l => `

    ${clean(l)}

    `) 203 204 return paragrafos.length > 0 ? paragrafos.join("\n") : "

    Projeto desenvolvido sob medida para atender às necessidades específicas do cliente.

    " 205 } 206 207 export function extractPrazoTotal(content: string): string { 208 const m1 = content.match(/(?:prazo|total|estimado)[:\s]+(\d+(?:[,.]\d+)?\s*(?:semanas?|meses?|dias?))/i) 209 if (m1) return `

input.description: Check total lines in HTML template

content.text: Let me explore a bit more about the current structure and then create the implementation plan.

Based on my comprehensive exploration of the codebase, I'll now create a detailed implementation plan to change the "Salvar PDF" button behavior from opening HTML in a new tab to directly downloading a PDF file.

## Implementation Plan: Direct PDF Download

### Current System Analysis

**Current Flow:**
1. `handleDownloadPDF()` fetches HTML template (`/proposta-template.html`)
2. Replaces template tokens using `buildProposalTokens()`
3. Opens new browser window with processed HTML
4. Triggers browser print dialog
5. User manually saves as PDF

**Key Assets:**
- **jsPDF library**: Already installed (`jspdf@^2.5.1`)
- **Token system**: Well-structured in `/lib/proposal-pdf-utils.ts`
- **HTML template**: 553-line print-optimized template at `/public/proposta-template.html`
- **Styling**: Comprehensive CSS with print-specific optimizations

### Recommended Technical Approach

**Option A: HTML-to-PDF using jsPDF + html2canvas** ✅ **RECOMMENDED**

**Why this approach:**
1. **Preserve existing layout**: Converts the current HTML template directly to PDF
2. **Minimal code changes**: Leverage existing token system and template
3. **Client-side**: Maintains current architecture pattern
4. **Visual fidelity**: Preserves fonts, colors, and layout exactly as designed
5. **No server dependency**: Works within current infrastructure

### Implementation Strategy

#### Phase 1: Add Required Dependencies
```bash
npm install html2canvas@^1.4.1
# Optional: html2pdf.js as alternative (wrapper around html2canvas + jsPDF)
npm install html2pdf.js@^0.10.1
```

#### Phase 2: Create PDF Generation Utility
**New file:** `/lib/pdf-generator.ts`
- HTML-to-PDF conversion function
- Error handling for canvas rendering
- PDF styling and page break optimization
- Filename generation utility

#### Phase 3: Modify Proposal Document Component
**File:** `/components/proposal-document.tsx`
- Replace current `handleDownloadPDF()` implementation
- Add PDF loading state management
- Implement error handling with user feedback
- Update button text from "Imprimir / Salvar PDF" to "Baixar PDF"

#### Phase 4: Optimize HTML Template for PDF
**File:** `/public/proposta-template.html`
- Ensure proper page break handling
- Optimize font loading for PDF rendering
- Add PDF-specific CSS media queries if needed

### Detailed Implementation Steps

#### Step 1: Install Dependencies
```bash
npm install html2canvas@^1.4.1 html2pdf.js@^0.10.1
```

#### Step 2: Create PDF Generator Utility
Create `/lib/pdf-generator.ts` with:
- `generatePDFFromHTML(htmlContent: string, filename: string): Promise<void>`
- Canvas configuration for high-quality rendering
- PDF page format (A4) and margins configuration
- Error handling for large documents

#### Step 3: Update Token System (Optional Enhancement)
Modify `/lib/proposal-pdf-utils.ts` to:
- Add PDF-specific optimizations
- Ensure proper image handling for canvas rendering
- Optimize long text sections for PDF layout

#### Step 4: Replace handleDownloadPDF Function
In `/components/proposal-document.tsx`:
- Import new PDF generator
- Create invisible div with processed HTML
- Render HTML to canvas using html2canvas
- Convert canvas to PDF using jsPDF
- Trigger automatic download
- Clean up temporary DOM elements

#### Step 5: Update UI/UX
- Change button text to "Baixar PDF"
- Update loading message to "Gerando PDF..."
- Improve error messages for PDF generation failures
- Add success toast with filename

### Technical Implementation Details

#### PDF Generation Flow:
1. **Prepare HTML**: Use existing token replacement system
2. **Create temporary container**: Render HTML in hidden div
3. **Canvas rendering**: html2canvas processes the HTML
4. **PDF generation**: jsPDF converts canvas to PDF
5. **Download trigger**: Automatic file download
6. **Cleanup**: Remove temporary DOM elements

#### Error Handling Strategy:
- Canvas rendering failures
- Large document size limits
- Memory constraints
- Browser compatibility issues
- Network timeouts

#### Performance Considerations:
- Lazy load html2canvas only when needed
- Implement progress feedback for large documents
- Optimize image processing
- Memory management for canvas operations

### Code Architecture Changes

#### New Dependencies:
- `html2canvas`: HTML element to canvas conversion
- `html2pdf.js`: Wrapper library (alternative approach)

#### Modified Files:
1. `/components/proposal-document.tsx` - Core PDF logic
2. `/lib/pdf-generator.ts` - New utility file
3. `/package.json` - New dependencies

#### Enhanced Features:
- Automatic filename: `Proposta_${empresa}_${date}.pdf`
- Better error handling with user-friendly messages
- Progress indicators for PDF generation
- Consistent visual output with current HTML template

### Testing Strategy

#### Unit Tests:
- PDF generator utility functions
- Token replacement accuracy
- Error handling scenarios

#### Integration Tests:
- End-to-end PDF generation
- Different proposal content types
- Browser compatibility (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)

#### User Acceptance Tests:
- PDF visual fidelity vs HTML template
- Download functionality across browsers
- Performance with large proposals

### Risk Mitigation

#### Potential Issues:
1. **Canvas size limits**: Large documents may exceed browser limits
2. **Font rendering**: Custom fonts may not render correctly in canvas
3. **Performance**: Heavy DOM manipulation for complex proposals
4. **Memory usage**: Large canvas operations

#### Mitigation Strategies:
1. **Page chunking**: Split large documents into multiple canvas renders
2. **Font preloading**: Ensure fonts are fully loaded before rendering
3. **Lazy loading**: Load PDF libraries only when needed
4. **Memory cleanup**: Proper disposal of canvas and temporary elements

### Alternative Approaches Considered

#### Option B: Pure jsPDF Layout Recreation
- **Pros**: Lightweight, full control
- **Cons**: Requires recreating entire layout, time-intensive

#### Option C: Server-side PDF Generation
- **Pros**: Better performance, more reliable
- **Cons**: Requires backend changes, server resources

#### Option D: Browser Print API Enhancement
- **Pros**: Minimal changes
- **Cons**: Still requires user interaction

### Success Metrics

1. **Functionality**: One-click PDF download without user intervention
2. **Quality**: PDF visually identical to current HTML output
3. **Performance**: PDF generation completes within 5 seconds for typical proposals
4. **Reliability**: 99%+ success rate across modern browsers
5. **User Experience**: Clear feedback during generation process

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `c:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Severino\components\proposal-document.tsx`
- `c:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Severino\lib\proposal-pdf-utils.ts`
- `c:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Severino\public\proposta-template.html`
- `c:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Severino\package.json`
- `c:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Severino\lib\pdf-generator.ts` (new file)

This implementation plan provides a comprehensive approach to converting the current HTML-based PDF workflow into a direct PDF download feature while maintaining visual fidelity and leveraging existing infrastructure.
